1.Title: Response Time Fluctuates Under Stable Load
1.Insight: For Homepage, despite [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remaining constant and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) increasing, response time showed significant fluctuation, indicating possible backend resource contention or intermittent bottlenecks.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained steady at 10 from 9:24 PM UTC to 9:26 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) climbed from 3.0 at 9:24 PM UTC to a peak of **6.2 requests/sec** by 9:25 PM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 3430 ms at 9:24 PM UTC to 1890 ms at 9:25 PM UTC, spiked to 2840 ms at 9:25 PM UTC, then fluctuated between **1840 ms and 2310 ms** from 9:26 PM UTC to 9:26 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider investigating underlying server health or resource limits during load fluctuations, focusing on database or API latency and potential saturation points when RPS spikes.

2.Title: Transient Response Time Spikes Observed
2.Insight: A brief spike in response time for [ALT-Sampler-3da67308-8367-4179-a4c5-ed67f5ae3e30](#ResourceLink) coincided with the highest [requests per second](#ClientMetrics), implying potential backend strain during load surges.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) peaked at **6.2 requests/sec** at 9:25 PM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) spiked from 1890 ms at 9:25 PM UTC to **2840 ms at 9:25 PM UTC**, despite [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remaining stable at 10.
2.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server-side performance and autoscaling triggers at moments of peak traffic, and investigate application logs for delays or exceptions during those times.

3.Title: Stable Virtual Users, Variable RPS
3.Insight: With [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) held constant, variations in [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) indicate potential client-side pacing or backend responsiveness issues for [ALT-Sampler-3da67308-8367-4179-a4c5-ed67f5ae3e30](#ResourceLink).
3.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) maintained at 10 between 9:24 PM UTC–9:26 PM UTC, dropped to 8 at 9:26 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) ranged from 3.0 to **6.2 requests/sec**, then fluctuated between 4.8 and 5.9 requests/sec after 9:26 PM UTC.
3.Recommendation: Consider auditing client test configuration for pacing logic and monitoring backend throughput limits. If backend metrics become available, correlate request handling latency during these fluctuations.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Client-side response times fluctuated between 1,840 ms and 3,430 ms, indicating latency spikes likely due to backend constraints.
